If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.64-0 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** No **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/87705 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** sdsodijnosdjoij@gmail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Exploratory - Significant User Experience Deterioration **Device used:** Mac 26.2 / Chrome **App Component:** Money Requests ## Action Performed: Precondition: - Enable Rules in workspace settings. - Enable Attendee tracking in Rules. 1. Go to staging.new.expensify.com 2. Go to workspace chat. 3. Click + > Create expense > Manual. 4. Enter amount > Next. 5. On confirm page, click Attendees. 6. Add "banana", then add "apple". 7. Click Create expense. 8. Create one more expense (no need add attendee). 9. Open the report. 10. Bulk select both expenses. 11. Click dropdown button > Merge. 12. Select the 3 attendees in Attendees section, then select any for other sections. 13. Click Continue. 14. On confirm page, hover over Attendees field and click Copy button. 15. Close the RHP. 16. Paste the content. ## Expected Result: Attendees will be copied in alphabetical order because they are arranged alphabetically on confirm page. ## Actual Result: Attendees are not copied in alphabetical order when they are arranged alphabetically on confirm page. title: [$250] Require a magic code when provisioning a new company card

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
Coming from a discussion in [this PR](https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/88345#discussion_r3148993082). # Problem A bad actor can work to gain control of an account from an unsuspecting user by adding the attacker's contact method as a secondary contact method during the company card provisioning flow (it's in the `WorkspaceCompanyCardAddWorkEmailPage` component), then setting that to the primary contact method (to receive future magic codes). ### Solution - Prompt the user for a magic code (we have this same flow in many different places in the app, so you can use those for inspiration) - Send that code to the API for setting the new default contact method - It's fine to assume for now that these requests will always be successful, even if an invalid code is entered. Eventually, the backend will start verifying the codes and return an error message if it isn't successful cc @rafecolton @shubham1286agra
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @shubham1206agra

Navigate to Account → Wallet 2. Select an existing bank account to continue setup 3. Proceed through the flow until Step 5 (Director information) 4. Select “No” for being a director 5. Enter a director’s email address 6. Submit and observe behavior ## Expected Result: User should: Successfully proceed to the next step after entering a valid director email All input fields should: Be trimmed (remove leading/trailing whitespace) before API submission ## Actual Result: User receives: Generic error: “Oops, an error has occurred” Flow does not proceed despite valid input ## Note: It seems like this bug is caused by an extra whitespace in the bankPostal field in the flow not being trimmed before being sent to the API, and we want to fix this in all the fields (bankPostal, bankCity, bankRegion, bankAddressLine1, bankAddressLine2, bankName, accountNumber} ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title:
[$250] Bullet lists render extra bullet from orphaned
and have suboptimal spacing

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Bug: HTML bullet lists render an extra bullet and have suboptimal spacing **Slack thread:** https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C090U774ZH7/p1777346806172969 ### Problem When Concierge sends HTML containing `
    /
  • ` bullet lists, two issues appear: 1. **Extra bullet:** An empty extra bullet renders at the end of the list 2. **Spacing:** The overall styling/spacing of bullet lists feels off ### Root Cause (Extra Bullet) Concierge sends HTML like: ```html
    • Item 1
    • Item 2
    • Item 3

    ``` Note the `
    ` tag inside the `
      ` but outside any `
    • `. The rendering pipeline converts `
        /
      • ` to custom `/` tags via ExpensiMark, but the orphaned `
        ` remains inside the `` container. The react-native-render-html library then renders this as an additional empty bullet. ### Relevant Code - **ExpensiMark** (in `expensify-common`): Transforms `
          /
        • ` → `/` — may need to strip orphaned `
          ` tags inside `
            ` during transformation - **`src/components/HTMLEngineProvider/BaseHTMLEngineProvider.tsx`**: Defines custom element models for `bullet-list` and `bullet-item`, and has existing whitespace filtering logic (`domVisitors.onText`) that prevents empty bullets from whitespace — but doesn't handle `
            ` tags - **`src/components/HTMLEngineProvider/HTMLRenderers/BulletItemRenderer.tsx`**: Renders individual bullet items with `CONST.DOT_SEPARATOR` (•) ### Expected Behavior - The `
            ` inside `
              ` should not create an extra bullet - Bullet list spacing should follow design recommendations ### Suggested Fix Either: 1. Strip `
              ` tags that are direct children of `
                ` during the ExpensiMark HTML-to-custom-tag transformation 2. Add a `domVisitors.onElement` handler in `BaseHTMLEngineProvider.tsx` to remove `
                ` nodes that are direct children of `` The spacing issue should be addressed with design input on the correct `marginVertical`, `paddingLeft`, and line-height values for `bullet-list` and `bullet-item`.
                Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @aimane-chnaif
                Upwork Automation - Do Not Edit
                • Upwork Job URL: https://www.upwork.com/jobs/~022048969288184722028
                • Upwork Job ID: 2048969288184722028
                • Last Price Increase: 2026-04-28
                • Automatic offers:
                  • aimane-chnaif | Reviewer | 111254189

If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:**v9.3.62-7 **Reproducible in staging?:** Y **Reproducible in production?:** Y **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** N/A **Logs:** [Logs](https://www.expensify.com/_devportal/tools/logSearch/#query=request_id:=%25229efe823f0e17bdba-YVR%2522+_time:%5B2026-04-21T17:38:58.464Z,+2026-04-21T19:38:58.464Z%5D&index=victorialogs&engine=victorialogs) **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:**https://github.com/Expensify/Expensify/issues/626851#issuecomment-4307515474 **Issue reported by:** @NicMendonca **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#quality](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C05LX9D6E07/p1776862240778499) ## Action Performed: Pre-requisites: - Control workspace - ADVANCED approval - userA submits to userB, userB forwardsTo userC 1. userA submit report to userB 2. userB approves and forwards the report to userC 3. Observe the expense fields on the report aren't editable by userA 4. Admin changes the approver of userA to userC 5. Observe the expense fields on the report are now editable by userA 6. userA edits an expense field and hits an error. ## Expected Result: The expenses on the report shouldn't become editable after the approval workflow update. ## Actual Result: When the approval workflow is updated such that the forwardsTo now becomes the submitter's submitsTo, the front-end incorrectly allows editing fields, but the backend blocks it and returns an error. Note: Melvin seems to think this is a mismatch with logic in `getSubmitToAccountID` and `isAwaitingFirstLevelApproval` ## Workaround: N/A ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [x] Android: App - [x] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [x] iOS: mWeb Safari - [x] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos My repro: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5b75ec6b-f712-449d-8522-f4e5854740c6 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.62-12 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** No **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/85234 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** applausetester+khcategory20@applause.expensifail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Exploratory - Significant User Experience Deterioration **Device used:** Mac 26.2 / Chrome **App Component:** Workspace Settings ## Action Performed: Precondition: - Workspace is set up with Expensify card feature. - Assign a virtual card to any member. 1. Go to staging.new.expensify.com 2. Go to workspace settings > Expensify Card. 3. Click on any card. 4. Click View transactions. 5. Click Workspaces tab. ## Expected Result: Workspaces tab will open workspace settings > Expensify Card where it was left off. ## Actual Result: Workspaces tab opens workspace list. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [x] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [x] iOS: mWeb Safari - [x] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/43d6d261-7354-4170-84b7-e52b252e2787 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
